DECLARATION OF JEFFREY M CURTISS IN SUPPORT OF OPPOS TO DEFT EVEREST NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO TRANSFER ACTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF COUNTY OF SAN BERNARDINO SAN BERNARDINO COUNTY FLOOD CONTROL DISTRICT

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT EVEREST NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO TRANSFER ACTION TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY IS GRANTED PER STIPULATION OF THE PARTIES. THE BASIS THAT VENUE IS PROPER IN SAN FRANCISCO IS THE PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS FOR DEFENDANT C.V. STARR & CO. ("STARR"). (OPP., P. 4:20.) IN DETERMINING WHETHER VENUE IS PROPER, THE COURT MAY CONSIDER EVIDENCE. (3 WITKIN, CAL. PROC. (5TH ED. 2008) ACTIONS, 895, P. 1126.) BASED ON THE STIPULATION OF PLAINTIFFS AND DEFENDANT EVEREST NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY ("EVEREST"), DEFENDANT STARR IS IMPROPERLY JOINED AS A DEFENDANT IN THE FIRST, SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. DEFENDANT STARR HAD NO ROLE IN THE DENIAL OF THE CLAIM AT ISSUE IN THIS ACTION. (CCP 395(B).) FOR THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANT EVEREST HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE THAT IN ITS ROLE AS UNDERWRITER, DEFENDANT STARR DID NOT ENGAGE IN THE ALLEGED ANY ADVERTISING OR CORRESPONDENCE THAT DEFENDANT EVEREST WOULD PROMPTLY PAY FOR LARGE LOSSES. (SEE GERVASIO DECL., 12.) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED ANY EVIDENCE TO THE CONTRARY, AND RELIES SOLELY ON THE ALLEGATIONS OF ITS SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. (OPP., P. 14:12 - 15:5.) THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES AND ENDS OF JUSTICE WOULD BE PROMOTED BY TRANSFER. (CCP 397(C).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-10-2014 Continued To May-07-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Action
Off Calendar Feb-11-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JEFFREY CURTIS OF THE STANZLER LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. (650) 739-0200 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. STEVEN P. RICE OF THE CROWELL & MORING LLP, TEL. NO. (949) 263-8400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT EVEREST NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY;
ATTY. K. STEPHEN TANG OF THE KNAPP, PETERESEN & CLARKE, TEL. NO. (818) 547-5000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT C.V. STARR & CO. BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court modified its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EVEREST NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Transfer Action to Los Angeles County is Granted per stipulation of the parties. The basis that venue is proper in San Francisco is the principal place of business for Defendant C.V. Starr & Co. ("Starr"). (Opp., p. 4:20.) In determining whether venue is proper, the Court may consider evidence. (3 Witkin, Cal. Proc. (5th ed. 2008) Actions, 895, p. 1126.) Based on the stipulation of Plaintiffs and Defendant Everest National Insurance Company ("Everest"), Defendant Starr is improperly joined as a defendant in the first, second and third causes of action. Defendant Starr had no role in the denial of the Claim at issue in this action. (CCP 395(b).) For the fourth cause of action, Defendant Everest has presented evidence that in its role as underwriter, Defendant Starr did not engage in the alleged any advertising or correspondence that Defendant Everest would promptly pay for large losses. (See Gervasio Decl., 12.) Plaintiff has not presented any evidence to the contrary, and relies solely on the allegations of its Second Amended Complaint. (Opp., p. 14:12 - 15:5.) The convenience of witnesses and ends of justice would be promoted by transfer. (CCP 397(c).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976
